tasks.reasoning:
Step 1: Understand the reported bug or failing test
This is one of the most crucial steps in debugging, as it helps the team understand the problem at a high level. Determine what is causing the issue and why the test is failing. This knowledge will be crucial when making modifications to code.

Step 2: Identify the root cause by reading relevant code
Once you have identified the problem, take a closer look at the relevant code. Look for specific lines or methods that are causing the issue. Take note of any patterns or behaviors that may be related. This will help in making more targeted and efficient modifications to code.

Step 3: Make minimal, targeted fixes
The root cause is usually a specific piece of code, but sometimes it's just one line of code that needs to be changed. In this case, it would be best to focus on the affected lines or methods. This will help prevent other tests from becoming corrupted or broken as well.

Step 4: Add test cases to prevent regression
In addition to making minimal fixes, make sure to also add test cases that verify the fix works and doesn't break other tests. This will prevent the fix from causing unexpected or undesirable behavior.

Step 5: Verify the fix doesn't break other tests
Before submitting a fix, ensure that it doesn't break any existing tests. This is crucial as if it does, it may need to be reverted or modified to avoid future issues.

Provide an action plan:
1. Main goal - Identify the problem and root cause
2. Key files to modify - The affected code(s) in shared/chat_memory.py
3. Step-by-step implementation plan - Conduct thorough code review of relevant code, identify specific lines or methods that are causing the issue, make minimal, targeted fixes, add test cases to prevent regression, and verify fix doesn't break other tests before submitting a fix. 4. Validation approach - Regression testing on existing functionality with new code to confirm that fix works correctly. Risks may include unexpected behavior or issues with integration with other codebases.
